title: Nonreciprocal photon bundle emission
authors: Baijun Li, Jing-Xue Liu, Tian-Xiang Lu, Le-Man Kuang, Chaohong Lee, Hui Jing
abstract: Quantum squeezing, a cornerstone of quantum optics and photonics, has played a key role in achieving ultra-precision sensing and realizing nonreciprocal engineering. However, the nonreciprocal multiquanta emission has remained largely unexplored by using directional quantum squeezing. Here, the one-way photon-photon bundle emission in a compound system consisted of two coupled optical resonators and a two-level atom is investigated. It is found that the directional quantum squeezing induces the asymmetric frequency detuning and photon hopping interaction between the two resonators, leading to the directional excitation of the two-photon super-Rabi oscillation. In particular, by harnessing intrinsic dissipation of the system, two types of two-photon bundle emission can be selectively induced for the probe field input from one direction while it is prohibited with the probe from the other direction. This finding bridges the broad fields ranging from nonreciprocal physics to quantum squeezing optics and multiquanta emission control through an all-optical approach, which can enable potential applications in chiral quantum emitters and backscattering-immune photonic communications.
summary: The paper explores generating directional two-photon photon bundles using a coupled resonator system driven by quantum squeezing. By analyzing the system's dynamics under different input directions, the authors demonstrate that squeezing breaks symmetry, enabling emission only from one direction. This offers an all-optical method to control chiral quantum light sources.
main_problem: The study investigates nonreciprocal photon bundle emission, a phenomenon controlled by directional quantum squeezing.
main_result: Directional quantum squeezing selectively induces two-photon bundle emission when the probe field enters from one direction while prohibiting it from the opposite direction.
method: The dynamics are analyzed using Hamiltonian derivations and master equations, supplemented by Bogoliubov transformations and correlation function calculations.
model_or_system: The system is a compound setup involving two coupled optical resonators (microcavities) and a two-level atom within the context of quantum optics. The interaction is modeled via an effective Hamiltonian incorporating squeezing parameters.
key_observables: Two-photon super-Rabi oscillation, second-order correlation functions ($g^{(2)}$), and photon populations in specific quantum states.
important_parameters: Squeezing parameter (r), detuning ($\Delta$), cavity decay rates ($\kappa$), and atomic decay rate ($\gamma$).
main_assumptions: The rotating-wave approximation is used, and thermal effects are neglected due to high resonance frequencies.
figures_summary: Figures schematically show the setup with input ports (Port 1/2) and pumping mechanisms. They illustrate super-Rabi oscillations via population dynamics and confirm nonreciprocity using $g^{(2)}$ correlation functions for different input directions.
paper_structure: The paper establishes the physical model, derives Hamiltonians for left/right inputs, analyzes the resulting super-Rabi oscillations under dissipation, and finally uses correlation functions to quantify the directional asymmetry of the photon bundle emission.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant as it combines quantum squeezing (a key tool in AMO) with nonreciprocal effects and multiquanta light generation, providing an all-optical control mechanism for quantum sources.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic of nonreciprocal two-photon bundle emission via directional quantum squeezing. (a,b) Compound system comprises two coupled whispering-gallery mode microcavities and a two-level atom, where a weak probe field is input from port 1 or from port 2. (c,d) Cavity b, containing χ(2) nonlinearity, is driven by a strong coherent laser from port 3, generating the directed quantum squeezing. A strong pump field also is applied to the two-level atom. The directed squeezing leads to an asymmetric detuning and photon hopping interaction in the system. The physical origin of the nonreciprocal two-photon bundle emission. Figures (a) and (b) depict the populations of the states |00+⟩, |11−⟩, and |20−⟩as a function of scaled evolution time ξt, when the probe field is input from the left side and the right side, respectively. (c-e) Quantum trajectory of the populations of the states |nm±⟩(n, m = 0, 1) for the probe field input from left side.
